metadata.title: Manikanta Engalligi — Portfolio
metadata.description: Manikanta Engalligi — AI Engineer | Full-Stack Developer | Data Analysis. Building intelligent systems, scalable web applications and data-driven solutions.
metadata.keywords: Manikanta Engalligi, AI Engineer, Full-Stack Developer, Data Analysis, Machine Learning, Next.js, TypeScript, React

hero.name: Manikanta Engalligi
hero.headline: AI Engineer | Full-Stack Developer | Data Analysis
hero.intro: Building intelligent systems, scalable web applications, and data-driven solutions.

summary.title: Professional Summary
summary.intro: I'm Manikanta Engalligi — a Master's student in Computer Science specializing in Big Data & Artificial Intelligence at SRH University Leipzig, Germany.
summary.p1: I build scalable software across full-stack development, artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data analytics, turning complex ideas into reliable, production-ready systems.
summary.p2: I'm driven by solving real-world problems with technology and continuously sharpening my craft through modern software engineering practices.

skills.title: Skills
skills.subtitle: A curated overview of the programming languages, frameworks, platforms and engineering tools I use across AI, Full-Stack Development, Data Analytics and Cloud-based applications. Select any technology to explore where it was applied.

python.summary: My primary language for machine learning and data work — I used it to build complete ML pipelines end to end.
python.outcome: Delivered two complete ML systems for anomaly and spam detection.

java.summary: Object-oriented programming foundation from my computer science studies and coursework projects.

projects.title: Projects
projects.subtitle: Selected engineering case studies across Full-Stack, AI, Computer Vision, Machine Learning and IoT.

data-analysis-toolkit.title: Data Analysis Toolkit
data-analysis-toolkit.subtitle: Full-stack, AI-assisted analytics platform for interactive data exploration
data-analysis-toolkit.description: A modular full-stack toolkit for statistical analysis, data visualization and AI-assisted insights through interactive dashboards.
data-analysis-toolkit.overview: Data Analysis Toolkit is my latest flagship project — a modern full-stack platform that turns raw datasets into interactive insights. It combines a responsive analytics dashboard, statistical analysis and AI-assisted exploration within a modular, scalable architecture.
features: Interactive dashboards with dynamic, responsive charts
Statistical analysis: descriptive statistics, correlations and distributions
AI-assisted analytics for automated insights and summaries
Flexible data import and preprocessing
Reusable, modular analysis components
Scalable design built for growing datasets

architecture.name: Frontend
architecture.description: A React and Next.js dashboard built with TypeScript and Tailwind CSS for a responsive, type-safe interface.

architecture.name: Backend / API
architecture.description: A REST API layer (Node.js / Express) that orchestrates data processing and serves analysis results to the frontend.

architecture.name: Analytics Engine
architecture.description: Python-powered statistical and machine-learning routines that perform computation and generate AI-assisted insights.

architecture.name: Modular Design
architecture.description: Decoupled modules for ingestion, processing, visualization and insight generation, keeping the system scalable and maintainable.

modules.name: Data Ingestion
modules.description: Imports and validates datasets, preparing them for analysis.

modules.name: Statistical Analysis
modules.description: Computes descriptive statistics, correlations and distributions.

modules.name: Visualization Engine
modules.description: Renders interactive, responsive charts and dashboards.

modules.name: AI Insights
modules.description: Generates automated summaries and AI-assisted observations.

modules.name: API Layer
modules.description: Connects the frontend dashboard with backend processing through REST endpoints.

contributions: Designed a modular, scalable full-stack architecture.
Developed an interactive analytics dashboard with reusable components.
Implemented statistical analysis and data-visualization features.
Integrated AI-assisted analytics into the exploration workflow.
Built a clean REST API layer between the frontend and processing logic.
Optimized the interface for responsiveness and performance.
challenges: Designing a modular structure that scales as datasets and features grow.
Presenting complex statistical results in a clear, interactive UI.
Keeping the analytics workflow responsive for larger datasets.
outcomes: A flagship full-stack toolkit showcasing end-to-end data-analytics engineering.
Reusable analysis modules and dashboards adaptable to new datasets.
A scalable foundation ready for additional AI-assisted features.

vital-guard.title: Vital Guard – IoT Based Safety System for Seniors and Disabled
vital-guard.subtitle: Wearable IoT device with automatic fall detection and real-time caregiver alerts
vital-guard.description: An IoT wearable that detects falls using ESP8266 and MPU6050, then sends real-time, GPS-located alerts to caregivers via Blynk.
vital-guard.overview: Vital Guard is a wearable IoT safety system that protects seniors and people with disabilities by automatically detecting falls and notifying caregivers in real time with the wearer's live location.
features: Automatic fall detection using threshold-based motion analysis
Real-time caregiver notifications via the Blynk IoT platform
Live GPS location tracking of the wearer
Emergency alerts triggered on detected falls
False-alarm cancellation to reduce unnecessary alerts
Hospital lookup support for nearby medical help

architecture.name: Sensor Layer
architecture.description: An MPU6050 accelerometer/gyroscope captures motion data while a GPS module provides the wearer's live location.

architecture.name: Microcontroller (ESP8266)
architecture.description: Reads sensor data, runs the fall-detection algorithm and manages Wi-Fi communication.

architecture.name: Fall Detection Logic
architecture.description: Threshold-based motion detection identifies sudden impacts and abnormal motion patterns.

architecture.name: Cloud / IoT Layer (Blynk)
architecture.description: Receives events over Wi-Fi and delivers real-time alerts and dashboards to caregivers.

architecture.name: Notification Layer
architecture.description: Pushes emergency alerts together with the GPS location to the caregiver's device.

modules.name: Fall Detection Module
modules.description: Analyzes MPU6050 motion data against thresholds to detect falls.

modules.name: GPS Tracking
modules.description: Continuously captures the wearer's location for emergency response.

modules.name: Alert System
modules.description: Triggers and sends emergency notifications to caregivers in real time.

modules.name: False Alarm Cancellation
modules.description: Lets the wearer cancel accidental triggers to avoid false alerts.

modules.name: Cloud Communication
modules.description: Transmits events to the Blynk platform over Wi-Fi.

modules.name: Hospital Lookup Support
modules.description: Helps locate nearby hospitals for faster assistance.

contributions: Designed the end-to-end IoT architecture across hardware and cloud.
Implemented a threshold-based fall-detection algorithm on the ESP8266.
Integrated MPU6050 motion sensing with GPS location tracking.
Developed real-time caregiver alerts through the Blynk platform.
Added a false-alarm cancellation mechanism to improve reliability.
Tested the device across simulated fall and motion scenarios.
challenges: Tuning motion thresholds to detect falls reliably while minimizing false positives.
Maintaining stable Wi-Fi connectivity and cloud communication on a low-power device.
Coordinating sensor readings, GPS and alerts in real time.
outcomes: A working wearable prototype that detects falls and alerts caregivers with live location.
Reduced false alarms through threshold tuning and a cancellation mechanism.
Completed as my Final Year Project, demonstrating end-to-end IoT engineering.

video-anomaly-detection.title: Video Anomaly Detection using Transfer Learning & Fine-Tuning
video-anomaly-detection.subtitle: Deep-learning system for frame-level anomaly detection in surveillance video
video-anomaly-detection.description: A computer-vision system that detects anomalies in surveillance footage using CNNs, transfer learning and fine-tuning across benchmark datasets.
video-anomaly-detection.overview: This project detects abnormal events in surveillance video at the frame level using convolutional neural networks, applying transfer learning and fine-tuning to achieve strong performance with limited training data.
features: Frame-level anomaly detection in video streams
Transfer learning from pretrained CNN models
Fine-tuning to adapt models to surveillance data
Evaluation across multiple benchmark datasets
OpenCV-based frame extraction and preprocessing
Performance comparison between model configurations

architecture.name: Data Preprocessing
architecture.description: OpenCV extracts and normalizes video frames from each dataset for model input.

architecture.name: Feature Extraction (CNN)
architecture.description: Pretrained convolutional networks extract spatial features from video frames.

architecture.name: Transfer Learning
architecture.description: Pretrained weights bootstrap learning so the model performs well with limited data.

architecture.name: Fine-Tuning
architecture.description: Selected layers are retrained to specialize the model for anomaly detection.

architecture.name: Evaluation
architecture.description: Frame-level predictions are scored and compared using standard metrics.

modules.name: Dataset Preprocessing
modules.description: Loads and prepares UCSD Ped1/Ped2 and CUHK Avenue frames with OpenCV.

modules.name: Transfer Learning Pipeline
modules.description: Initializes models from pretrained CNN weights.

modules.name: Fine-Tuning Stage
modules.description: Retrains chosen layers to adapt the model to anomaly detection.

modules.name: Training Pipeline
modules.description: Manages model training, validation and checkpointing.

modules.name: Evaluation & Metrics
modules.description: Computes frame-level metrics and compares model performance.

contributions: Designed a transfer-learning pipeline for video anomaly detection.
Implemented frame extraction and preprocessing with OpenCV.
Fine-tuned CNN models on UCSD Ped1, Ped2 and CUHK Avenue datasets.
Evaluated models using frame-level anomaly metrics.
Compared performance across datasets and model configurations.
Optimized training to work effectively with limited labeled data.
challenges: Handling varied lighting, crowd density and camera conditions across datasets.
Balancing detection sensitivity against false alarms.
Achieving strong accuracy with limited labeled anomaly data.
outcomes: Frame-level anomaly detection across three benchmark datasets.
Demonstrated that transfer learning and fine-tuning improve results with limited data.
A documented comparison of model performance across datasets.

sms-spam-detection.title: SMS Spam Detection System
sms-spam-detection.subtitle: NLP and machine-learning pipeline for classifying SMS messages as spam or ham
sms-spam-detection.description: A machine-learning system that classifies SMS messages as spam or legitimate using TF-IDF features and trained classifiers.
sms-spam-detection.overview: An end-to-end NLP pipeline that cleans and vectorizes SMS text, then classifies each message as spam or legitimate using machine-learning models.
features: Text cleaning and preprocessing of raw SMS data
TF-IDF feature extraction from message text
Machine-learning based spam/ham classification
End-to-end prediction pipeline
Model evaluation on labeled data
Lightweight, fast inference

architecture.name: Data Preprocessing
architecture.description: Cleans raw SMS text through lowercasing, noise removal and tokenization.

architecture.name: Feature Extraction
architecture.description: Transforms cleaned text into numerical vectors using TF-IDF.

architecture.name: Model Training
architecture.description: Trains scikit-learn classifiers on labeled spam/ham data.

architecture.name: Prediction Pipeline
architecture.description: Applies preprocessing and the trained model to classify new messages.

architecture.name: Evaluation
architecture.description: Measures performance with standard classification metrics.

modules.name: Text Cleaning
modules.description: Normalizes and removes noise from raw SMS messages.

modules.name: Feature Engineering
modules.description: Builds TF-IDF feature vectors from the cleaned text.

modules.name: Classification Model
modules.description: Trains and applies the spam/ham classifier.

modules.name: Prediction Pipeline
modules.description: Wraps preprocessing and inference into a reusable flow.

modules.name: Model Evaluation
modules.description: Assesses accuracy and other classification metrics.

contributions: Designed an end-to-end NLP classification pipeline.
Implemented text cleaning and preprocessing routines.
Engineered TF-IDF features from SMS text.
Trained and evaluated machine-learning classifiers.
Built a reusable prediction pipeline for new messages.
challenges: Handling imbalanced spam and ham classes.
Avoiding overfitting on short, noisy text messages.
outcomes: Accurate spam/ham classification on labeled SMS data.
A reusable, lightweight NLP pipeline for fast inference.
A clear foundation for future deployment as an API.
